documentation.value:
Execute a block indefinitely until `break` is called.

```r
repeat {
  x <- x + 1
  if (x > 10) break
}
```

Used in `for` loops to iterate over sequences.

```r
for (x in 1:10) print(x)
```

Skip the rest of the current iteration and proceed to the next.

```r
for (i in 1:10) {
  if (i %% 2 == 0) next
  print(i)
}
```

Create a function object.

```r
my_func <- function(x, y = 1) {
  x + y
}

# Anonymous function
sapply(1:5, function(x) x^2)
```

Logical truth value.

```r
x <- TRUE
is.logical(TRUE)  # TRUE
```

Logical false value.

```r
x <- FALSE
!FALSE  # TRUE
```

The NULL object represents the absence of a value.

```r
x <- NULL
is.null(x)  # TRUE
length(NULL)  # 0
```

Logical constant representing a missing value.

```r
x <- c(1, NA, 3)
is.na(x)       # FALSE TRUE FALSE
na.rm = TRUE   # common argument to ignore NAs
```

Missing value of integer type.

```r
x <- NA_integer_
is.na(x)  # TRUE
typeof(x)  # "integer"
```

Missing value of double type.

```r
x <- NA_real_
typeof(x)  # "double"
```

Missing value of complex type.

```r
x <- NA_complex_
typeof(x)  # "complex"
```

Missing value of character type.

```r
x <- NA_character_
typeof(x)  # "character"
```

Positive infinity.

```r
1 / 0     # Inf
is.infinite(Inf)  # TRUE
```

Not a Number â€” result of undefined numeric operations.

```r
0 / 0      # NaN
is.nan(NaN)  # TRUE
```

Load and attach a package namespace.

```r
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
```

Try to load a package. Returns TRUE/FALSE instead of erroring.

```r
if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
```

completions.label: source
completions.detail: Read and evaluate R code from a file

documentation.value:
Parse and evaluate R expressions from a file.

```r
source("helper_functions.R")
source("config.R", local = TRUE)
```

Evaluate expression in a local environment.

```r
result <- local({
  tmp <- 42
  tmp * 2
})
```

Select one of several alternatives based on a value.

```r
result <- switch(x,
  "a" = 1,
  "b" = 2,
  "c" = 3,
  -1  # default
)
```

Generic print function for displaying objects.

```r
print(x)
print(df, n = 20)
print("hello")
```

Concatenate and print to stdout or a file.

```r
cat("Hello", "world\n")
cat("x =", x, "\n")
cat("line\n", file = "out.txt", append = TRUE)
```

Concatenate strings with a separator (default space).

```r
paste("Hello", "world")        # "Hello world"
paste("a", "b", sep = "-")     # "a-b"
paste0("x", 1:3)               # "x1" "x2" "x3"
```

Concatenate strings with no separator (equivalent to `paste(..., sep = "")`.

```r
paste0("file", 1:3, ".csv")  # "file1.csv" "file2.csv" "file3.csv"
paste0("prefix_", name)
```

C-style string formatting.

```r
sprintf("%s is %d years old", name, age)
sprintf("%.2f%%", pct * 100)
sprintf("%05d", id)
```

Combine values into a vector or list.

```r
x <- c(1, 2, 3)
names <- c("Alice", "Bob")
mixed <- c(a = 1, b = 2)
```

Get or set the length of a vector or list.

```r
length(1:10)         # 10
length(list(a=1))    # 1
length(NULL)         # 0
```

completions.label: nchar
completions.detail: Number of characters in a string

documentation.value:
Count the number of characters in a string.

```r
nchar("hello")     # 5
nchar(c("a", "bb"))  # 1 2
```

Generate regular sequences.

```r
seq(1, 10)             # 1 2 ... 10
seq(0, 1, by = 0.1)   # 0.0 0.1 ... 1.0
seq_len(5)             # 1 2 3 4 5
seq_along(x)           # 1:length(x)
```

Generate sequence `1:length.out`.

```r
seq_len(5)  # 1 2 3 4 5
```

Replicate elements of a vector.

```r
rep(0, 5)              # 0 0 0 0 0
rep(1:3, times = 2)    # 1 2 3 1 2 3
rep(1:3, each = 2)     # 1 1 2 2 3 3
```

Reverse the elements of a vector.

```r
rev(1:5)           # 5 4 3 2 1
rev(c("a", "b"))   # "b" "a"
```

Sort a vector in ascending or descending order.

```r
sort(c(3, 1, 2))                 # 1 2 3
sort(c(3, 1, 2), decreasing = TRUE)  # 3 2 1
```

Return a permutation which rearranges its argument into ascending or descending order.

```r
x <- c(3, 1, 2)
order(x)        # 2 3 1
x[order(x)]     # 1 2 3
df[order(df$age), ]
```

Return indices where a logical vector is TRUE.

```r
which(c(FALSE, TRUE, TRUE))  # 2 3
which(x > 5)
which.min(x)
which.max(x)
```

Return positions of first matches of first argument in second.

```r
match(c("b", "d"), c("a", "b", "c"))  # 2 NA
x %in% table  # logical vector
```

Return unique elements of a vector.

```r
unique(c(1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3))  # 1 2 3
```

Return a logical vector indicating which elements are duplicates.

```r
duplicated(c(1, 2, 2, 3))  # FALSE FALSE TRUE FALSE
x[!duplicated(x)]  # remove duplicates
```

Build a frequency table of factor levels.

```r
table(c("a", "b", "a", "c", "a"))  # a:3 b:1 c:1
table(df$gender, df$group)
```

Compute the sum of all values.

```r
sum(1:10)             # 55
sum(x, na.rm = TRUE)
```

Compute the arithmetic mean.

```r
mean(1:10)             # 5.5
mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
mean(x, trim = 0.1)
```

Compute the median value.

```r
median(c(1, 3, 5, 7, 9))  # 5
median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
```

Compute the standard deviation.

```r
sd(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5))  # 1.581139
sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
```

Compute variance (or covariance matrix).

```r
var(1:10)
var(x, y)     # covariance
var(df)       # covariance matrix
```

Return the minimum of all values.

```r
min(5, 3, 8)         # 3
min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
```

Return the maximum of all values.

```r
max(5, 3, 8)         # 8
max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
```

Return the minimum and maximum of a vector.

```r
range(c(3, 1, 5))  # 1 5
```

Compute the absolute value.

```r
abs(-5)    # 5
abs(c(-1, 2, -3))  # 1 2 3
```

Compute the square root.

```r
sqrt(16)   # 4
sqrt(1:5)
```

Compute the natural logarithm (or with specified base).

```r
log(exp(1))       # 1
log(100, base = 10)  # 2
```

Compute the base-2 logarithm.

```r
log2(8)   # 3
log2(1024)  # 10
```

Compute the base-10 logarithm.

```r
log10(1000)  # 3
```

Compute the exponential function e^x.

```r
exp(1)    # 2.718282
exp(0)    # 1
```

Round up to the nearest integer.

```r
ceiling(2.3)  # 3
ceiling(-2.3) # -2
```

Round down to the nearest integer.

```r
floor(2.7)  # 2
floor(-2.3) # -3
```

Round a number to n digits.

```r
round(3.14159, 2)  # 3.14
round(2.5)          # 2 (banker's rounding)
```

Truncate towards zero.

```r
trunc(2.9)   # 2
trunc(-2.9)  # -2
```

Return the sign: -1, 0, or 1.

```r
sign(-5)  # -1
sign(0)   # 0
sign(3)   # 1
```

Compute the cumulative sum.

```r
cumsum(1:5)  # 1 3 6 10 15
```

Compute the cumulative product.

```r
cumprod(1:5)  # 1 2 6 24 120
```

Compute the cumulative maxima.

```r
cummax(c(3, 1, 4, 1, 5))  # 3 3 4 4 5
```

Compute the cumulative minima.

```r
cummin(c(3, 1, 4, 1, 5))  # 3 1 1 1 1
```

Compute lagged differences.

```r
diff(c(1, 3, 6, 10))  # 2 3 4
diff(x, lag = 2)
```

Return the first n elements.

```r
head(1:100)        # 1 2 3 4 5 6
head(df, 10)
```

Return the last n elements.

```r
tail(1:100)        # 95 96 97 98 99 100
tail(df, 5)
```

Return the number of rows of a matrix or data frame.

```r
nrow(mtcars)  # 32
```

Return the number of columns of a matrix or data frame.

```r
ncol(mtcars)  # 11
```

Get or set the dimensions of an object.

```r
dim(mtcars)       # 32 11
dim(matrix(1:6, nrow = 2))  # 2 3
```

Get or set the names of an object.

```r
names(x)
names(x) <- c("a", "b", "c")
```

Get or set column names.

```r
colnames(df)
colnames(df) <- c("x", "y", "z")
```

Get or set row names.

```r
rownames(df)
rownames(df) <- paste0("row", 1:nrow(df))
```

Compactly display the internal structure.

```r
str(mtcars)
str(my_list)
```

Produce result summaries (dispatched by class).

```r
summary(mtcars)
summary(lm_fit)
summary(factor_var)
```

Get or set the class attribute.

```r
class(mtcars)    # "data.frame"
class(1:5)       # "integer"
```

Return the internal storage type.

```r
typeof(1L)       # "integer"
typeof(1.0)      # "double"
typeof("a")      # "character"
```

Test whether an object is numeric (integer or double).

```r
is.numeric(1.5)    # TRUE
is.numeric("a")    # FALSE
```

Test whether an object is of type character.

```r
is.character("hello")  # TRUE
is.character(42)       # FALSE
```

Test whether an object is of type logical.

```r
is.logical(TRUE)   # TRUE
is.logical(1)      # FALSE
```

Test each element for NA.

```r
is.na(c(1, NA, 3))  # FALSE TRUE FALSE
sum(is.na(x))       # count NAs
```

Test if an object is NULL.

```r
is.null(NULL)  # TRUE
is.null(NA)    # FALSE
```

Coerce an object to numeric (double).

```r
as.numeric("3.14")  # 3.14
as.numeric(TRUE)    # 1
```

Coerce an object to character string.

```r
as.character(42)     # "42"
as.character(TRUE)   # "TRUE"
```

Coerce to logical.

```r
as.logical(0)       # FALSE
as.logical(1)       # TRUE
as.logical("TRUE")  # TRUE
```

Coerce to integer.

```r
as.integer(3.7)    # 3
as.integer("42")   # 42
```

Coerce to double-precision floating point.

```r
as.double(1L)      # 1
as.double("3.14")  # 3.14
```

Coerce to a data.frame.

```r
as.data.frame(matrix(1:6, nrow = 2))
as.data.frame(my_list)
```

Create a data frame from column vectors.

```r
df <- data.frame(
  name = c("Alice", "Bob"),
  age = c(25, 30),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
```

Create a matrix from the given values.

```r
matrix(1:6, nrow = 2, ncol = 3)
matrix(0, nrow = 3, ncol = 3)
```

Create a multi-dimensional array.

```r
array(1:24, dim = c(2, 3, 4))
```

Create a list (generic container).

```r
x <- list(a = 1, b = "text", c = TRUE)
x[[1]]
x$a
```

Create a vector of given mode and length.

```r
vector("numeric", 5)     # 0 0 0 0 0
vector("character", 3)   # "" "" ""
```

Encode a vector as a factor (categorical variable).

```r
factor(c("low", "med", "high"), levels = c("low", "med", "high"), ordered = TRUE)
```

Read a CSV file into a data frame.

```r
df <- read.csv("data.csv")
df <- read.csv("data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE, na.strings = c("", "NA"))
```

Read a table formatted file into a data frame.

```r
df <- read.table("data.txt", header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
```

Write a data frame to a CSV file.

```r
write.csv(df, "output.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

Write a data frame to a file.

```r
write.table(df, "output.txt", sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE)
```

Read a single R object from file.

```r
obj <- readRDS("data.rds")
```

Serialize an R object to file.

```r
saveRDS(obj, "data.rds")
```

Construct platform-independent file paths.

```r
file.path("data", "input", "file.csv")
```

Test whether files exist.

```r
file.exists("data.csv")  # TRUE/FALSE
```

Create a directory.

```r
dir.create("output", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

Return the current working directory.

```r
getwd()  # "/home/user/project"
```

Change the working directory.

```r
setwd("/path/to/project")
```

Return the current date and time.

```r
Sys.time()  # "2024-01-15 14:30:00 UTC"
```

Suspend execution for a given number of seconds.

```r
Sys.sleep(2)  # pause 2 seconds
```

Invoke an OS command.

```r
system("ls -la")
output <- system("whoami", intern = TRUE)
```

Invoke an OS command (safer interface).

```r
system2("git", c("status", "--short"), stdout = TRUE)
```

Establish calling handlers for conditions.

```r
withCallingHandlers(
  expr,
  warning = function(w) invokeRestart("muffleWarning")
)
```

Stop execution and signal an error condition.

```r
stop("invalid argument")
stop("x must be > 0, got: ", x)
```

Generate a warning message.

```r
warning("deprecated function")
warning("NAs introduced by coercion", call. = FALSE)
```

Generate a diagnostic message (to stderr).

```r
message("Processing file: ", filename)
message("Step ", i, " of ", n)
```

Format an R object for pretty printing.

```r
format(3.14159, digits = 3)  # "3.14"
format(Sys.Date(), "%Y-%m-%d")
```

Extract or replace substrings.

```r
substr("Hello", 1, 3)  # "Hel"
```

Extract substrings (compatible with replacement).

```r
substring("Hello world", 7)  # "world"
```

Replace all occurrences of a pattern.

```r
gsub("o", "0", "foo bar boo")  # "f00 bar b00"
gsub("[[:space:]]+", "_", text)
```

Replace the first occurrence of a pattern.

```r
sub("o", "0", "foo")  # "f0o"
```

Search for pattern matches and return indices.

```r
grep("^a", c("apple", "banana", "avocado"))  # 1 3
grep("error", log_lines, value = TRUE)
```

Test for pattern matches, return logical vector.

```r
grepl("^a", c("apple", "banana"))  # TRUE FALSE
```

Find the first match of a pattern.

```r
m <- regexpr("[0-9]+", "abc123def")
regmatches("abc123def", m)  # "123"
```

Find all matches of a pattern.

```r
m <- gregexpr("[0-9]+", "a1b2c3")
regmatches("a1b2c3", m)  # list(c("1", "2", "3"))
```

Split character strings by a pattern.

```r
strsplit("a,b,c", ",")  # list(c("a", "b", "c"))
strsplit("hello", "")    # list(c("h","e","l","l","o"))
```

Remove leading and/or trailing whitespace.

```r
trimws("  hello  ")        # "hello"
trimws("  hello  ", "left")  # "hello  "
```

Convert strings to uppercase.

```r
toupper("hello")  # "HELLO"
```

Convert strings to lowercase.

```r
tolower("HELLO")  # "hello"
```

completions.label: startsWith
completions.detail: Test if string starts with prefix

documentation.value:
Test if strings start with a prefix.

```r
startsWith("Hello", "He")  # TRUE
```

completions.label: endsWith
completions.detail: Test if string ends with suffix

documentation.value:
Test if strings end with a suffix.

```r
endsWith("file.csv", ".csv")  # TRUE
```

Apply a function over rows or columns of a matrix.

```r
apply(mat, 1, sum)  # row sums
apply(mat, 2, mean) # column means
```

Apply a function and simplify the result.

```r
sapply(1:5, function(x) x^2)  # 1 4 9 16 25
sapply(df, class)
```

Apply a function over a list and return a list.

```r
lapply(1:3, function(x) x^2)  # list(1, 4, 9)
lapply(files, read.csv)
```

Apply with specified return type (safer than sapply).

```r
vapply(1:5, sqrt, numeric(1))
```

Apply a function to groups defined by an index.

```r
tapply(mtcars$mpg, mtcars$cyl, mean)
```

Apply a function to multiple lists/vectors element-wise.

```r
mapply(rep, 1:4, 4:1)  # rep(1,4), rep(2,3), etc.
```

Apply a function to corresponding elements (returns list).

```r
Map(`+`, 1:3, 4:6)  # list(5, 7, 9)
```

completions.label: Reduce
completions.detail: Reduce a list to a single value

documentation.value:
Apply a binary function cumulatively.

```r
Reduce(`+`, 1:5)  # 15
Reduce(merge, list_of_dfs)
```

Select elements for which a function returns TRUE.

```r
Filter(is.numeric, my_list)
Filter(function(x) x > 0, vec)
```

completions.label: do.call
completions.detail: Call a function with args list

documentation.value:
Execute a function call with arguments from a list.

```r
do.call(rbind, list_of_dfs)
do.call(paste, c(words, sep = "-"))
```

Create a vectorized version of a function.

```r
vfunc <- Vectorize(my_scalar_func)
vfunc(1:5)
```

Vectorized conditional: returns values depending on test.

```r
ifelse(x > 0, "pos", "non-pos")
ifelse(is.na(x), 0, x)
```

Generic function for plotting R objects.

```r
plot(x, y)
plot(1:10, main = "Title", xlab = "X", ylab = "Y", type = "l")
```

Draw a histogram.

```r
hist(rnorm(1000))
hist(x, breaks = 30, col = "steelblue", main = "Distribution")
```

Draw a bar plot.

```r
barplot(table(x), col = "steelblue")
barplot(height, names.arg = labels)
```

Produce box-and-whisker plots.

```r
boxplot(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars)
```

Add connected line segments to an existing plot.

```r
lines(x, y, col = "red", lty = 2)
```

Add points to an existing plot.

```r
points(x, y, col = "red", pch = 19)
```

completions.label: abline
completions.detail: Add straight line to a plot

documentation.value:
Add straight lines to a plot.

```r
abline(h = 0, col = "gray")
abline(v = mean(x), lty = 2)
abline(lm_fit, col = "red")
```

Add a legend to a plot.

```r
legend("topright", legend = c("A", "B"), col = c("red", "blue"), lty = 1)
```

Add annotations to a plot.

```r
title(main = "Main Title", sub = "Subtitle", xlab = "X", ylab = "Y")
```

Set or query graphical parameters.

```r
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))  # 2x2 plot layout
par(mar = c(5, 4, 4, 2) + 0.1)
```

Initialize a ggplot2 plot.

```r
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal()
```

Define aesthetic mappings for ggplot2.

```r
aes(x = var1, y = var2, color = group)
aes(fill = category, size = value)
```

Add a scatter plot layer.

```r
ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) + geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7)
```

Add a line plot layer.

```r
ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) + geom_line(color = "blue")
```

Add a bar chart layer.

```r
ggplot(df, aes(x = category)) + geom_bar()
ggplot(df, aes(x = cat, y = val)) + geom_bar(stat = "identity")
```

Facet a plot by a single variable.

```r
ggplot(df, aes(x, y)) + geom_point() + facet_wrap(~group)
```

Modify the non-data components of a plot.

```r
theme_minimal()
theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

Download and install packages from CRAN.

```r
install.packages("dplyr")
install.packages(c("ggplot2", "tidyr"))
```

Remove installed packages.

```r
remove.packages("old_pkg")
```

Load the namespace of a package without attaching it.

```r
ns <- loadNamespace("dplyr")
```

Return the version of an installed package.

```r
packageVersion("ggplot2")  # '3.4.4'
```

Update outdated packages.

```r
update.packages(ask = FALSE)
```

Return a matrix of available packages on CRAN.

```r
pkgs <- available.packages()
```

Fit a linear model using ordinary least squares.

```r
fit <- lm(y ~ x1 + x2, data = df)
summary(fit)
coef(fit)
```

Fit a generalized linear model.

```r
fit <- glm(y ~ x, data = df, family = binomial)
summary(fit)
```

Perform a t-test.

```r
t.test(x, mu = 0)
t.test(x, y, paired = TRUE)
```

Compute correlation between vectors or columns.

```r
cor(x, y)
cor(df, use = "complete.obs")
```

Test for association between paired samples.

```r
cor.test(x, y, method = "pearson")
```

Perform a chi-squared test.

```r
chisq.test(table(x, y))
```

Fit an analysis of variance model.

```r
fit <- aov(y ~ group, data = df)
summary(fit)
```

Compute analysis of variance tables for fitted model objects.

```r
anova(fit1)
anova(fit1, fit2)
```

Obtain predictions from a fitted model.

```r
predict(fit, newdata = test_df)
predict(fit, interval = "confidence")
```

Extract model residuals.

```r
residuals(fit)
resid(fit)  # alias
```

Extract fitted values from a model.

```r
fitted(fit)
```

Compute confidence intervals for model parameters.

```r
confint(fit)
confint(fit, level = 0.99)
```

Extract model coefficients.

```r
coef(fit)
```

Create an S4 class definition.

```r
setClass("Person", representation(
  name = "character",
  age = "numeric"
))
```

Create an S4 generic function.

```r
setGeneric("greet", function(object) standardGeneric("greet"))
```

Create a mutable reference class.

```r
Person <- setRefClass("Person",
  fields = list(name = "character", age = "numeric"),
  methods = list(
    greet = function() cat("Hello,", name, "\n")
  )
)
```

Create an R6 class (requires R6 package).

```r
library(R6)
Person <- R6Class("Person",
  public = list(
    name = NULL,
    initialize = function(name) self$name <- name,
    greet = function() cat("Hello,", self$name, "\n")
  )
)
p <- Person$new("Alice")
```

Create a new environment.

```r
e <- new.env(parent = emptyenv())
assign("x", 42, envir = e)
```

Get or set the environment of a function.

```r
environment(my_func)
environment()  # current environment
```

Return the global environment.

```r
globalenv()
ls(envir = globalenv())
```

Return the base environment.

```r
baseenv()
```

Return the empty environment (has no parent).

```r
emptyenv()
```

Return or set the parent (enclosing) environment.

```r
parent.env(e)
```

Check if a variable exists in an environment.

```r
exists("x")                    # TRUE/FALSE
exists("x", envir = my_env)
```

Retrieve a variable by name.

```r
get("x")
get("func", envir = ns)
```

Assign a value to a name in an environment.

```r
assign("x", 42)
assign("y", 100, envir = my_env)
```

Remove objects from an environment.

```r
rm(x, y)
rm(list = ls())  # clear all
```

List objects in an environment.

```r
ls()
ls(envir = my_env)
ls(pattern = "^data")
```

Attach a data frame or list to the search path.

```r
attach(mtcars)
mpg  # directly accessible
detach(mtcars)
```

Detach a previously attached object.

```r
detach(mtcars)
detach("package:dplyr")
```

Return the current search path.

```r
search()
# ".GlobalEnv" "package:stats" ...
```

completions.label: trycatch_block

documentation.value:
Read a CSV file into a data frame.

```r
df <- read.csv("data.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
str(df)
head(df)
```

completions.label: ggplot_snippet

documentation.value:
A quick ggplot2 scatter plot.

```r
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Title") +
  theme_minimal()
```

completions.insertText:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(${1:df}, aes(x = ${2:x}, y = ${3:y})) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "${4:Title}") +
  theme_minimal()

documentation.value:
Data transformation pipeline using the native pipe.

```r
library(dplyr)
result <- df |>
  filter(x > 0) |>
  mutate(y = x * 2) |>
  summarise(mean_y = mean(y))
```

completions.label: shiny_app

documentation.value:
Basic Shiny application template.

```r
library(shiny)
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("My App"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(sliderInput("n", "N:", 1, 100, 50)),
    mainPanel(plotOutput("plot"))
  )
)
server <- function(input, output) {
  output$plot <- renderPlot({ hist(rnorm(input$n)) })
}
shinyApp(ui, server)
```

completions.label: r6_class_snippet

documentation.value:
Integer type. Created with `L` suffix or `as.integer()`.

```r
x <- 42L
typeof(x)  # "integer"
```

Double-precision floating point number.

```r
x <- 3.14
typeof(x)  # "double"
```

Character (string) type.

```r
x <- "hello"
typeof(x)  # "character"
```

Logical type (TRUE/FALSE/NA).

```r
x <- TRUE
typeof(x)  # "logical"
```

Complex number type.

```r
x <- 1 + 2i
typeof(x)  # "complex"
```

Raw byte type.

```r
x <- charToRaw("A")
typeof(x)  # "raw"
```

Data frame â€” tabular data structure with named columns.

```r
df <- data.frame(x = 1:3, y = c("a", "b", "c"))
class(df)  # "data.frame"
```

Matrix â€” 2D array of same-type elements.

```r
m <- matrix(1:6, nrow = 2)
class(m)  # "matrix" "array"
```

Factor â€” categorical variable with fixed levels.

```r
f <- factor(c("low", "med", "high"))
class(f)  # "factor"
```

Environment â€” frame for variable bindings.

```r
e <- new.env()
is.environment(e)  # TRUE
```

Formula â€” symbolic model specification.

```r
f <- y ~ x1 + x2
class(f)  # "formula"
```

Expression â€” unevaluated R expressions.

```r
e <- expression(1 + 2)
eval(e)  # 3
```

Call â€” an unevaluated function call.

```r
cl <- quote(mean(x))
class(cl)  # "call"
```

Symbol â€” a variable name.

```r
s <- as.name("x")
class(s)  # "name"
```
